title: Stage 06 — Hook bench (swappable 3×3×3 components)
description: Typed I/O contract for /mkt-creative-run Stage 06 (design §2 — no free-text handoffs; §6.1 — typed stage boundaries). Stage 06 fans out a hook bench for one persona: independent hook / body / cta components that recombine (the 3×3×3 swappable-ID idea — three hooks × three bodies × three CTAs all carry their own stable id, so the rubric judge and live attention proxy can recombine and rank components rather than whole ads). Every copy-bearing field carries claim_refs; the ladder records the PRE-TEST predicted attention proxies + outcome targets (hypotheses, never asserted as proven — Stage-00 caution carried forward). provenance: run_id + persona_ref + generated_at.

claimRef.description: A reference to one approved/declared claim id in context/claims.json (e.g. "CLM-008"). The bare id only — the render markers [[CLM-008]] / {{CLM-008}} are presentation, not the ref. A claim_refs array lists every claim this copy asserts; the deterministic claims floor reads these to fail-close on unverified claims.

claimRefs.description: Every claim id this copy-bearing object asserts (may be empty when the copy makes no PrepEdu claim). Required on every copy-bearing object so the claims gate has an explicit, typed surface — never inferred from free text.

numberOrNull.description: A predicted/target number, or null when no target is set for this component (degrade-gracefully — never a fabricated figure).

run_id.description: Timestamped run id (e.g. 20260608-072919) — ties this artifact to the run manifest.

persona_ref.description: Persona this hook bench targets, referencing context/proposed/audience-personas.md (e.g. "Persona 4 — Thảo (Segment A)").

generated_at.description: ISO timestamp this stage artifact was produced.

hooks.description: The hook bench. Each entry binds one hook_id + body_id + cta_id, but those ids are INDEPENDENT and stable across the bench so components recombine (3×3×3). The judge ranks; nothing here is a proven winner.

hook_id.description: Stable id of the HOOK component (e.g. "H1"). Independent of body_id/cta_id so the same hook recombines with other bodies/CTAs across the bench.

body_id.description: Stable id of the BODY component (e.g. "B2"). Independent — recombines with other hooks/CTAs.

cta_id.description: Stable id of the CTA component (e.g. "C3"). Independent — recombines with other hooks/bodies.

hook_text.description: The hook copy as it appears on-screen / in-feed (locale-native, e.g. Vietnamese — never a translation). Copy-bearing → see claim_refs.

cta_text.description: The CTA copy (locale-native). Copy-bearing → its claims are included in claim_refs.

why_it_works.description: One-line rationale tracing the hook to the persona's motivator/force (the planning trace, not shipping copy).

formula.description: Copy formula the hook follows. AIDA (Attention-Interest-Desire-Action), PAS (Problem-Agitate-Solution), BAB (Before-After-Bridge), FAB (Features-Advantages-Benefits), or other.

ladder.description: PRE-TEST prediction ladder: attention proxies (thumb-stop / hold) the creative predicts, plus optional outcome targets. These are hypotheses for the live attention proxy + judge to test — they are NEVER presented as measured results (Stage-00 thin-high-CTR caution).

thumb_stop_pct_predicted.description: Predicted thumb-stop / 3s-view rate as a percentage (attention proxy, Tier-A). A prediction, not a measurement.

hold_pct_predicted.description: Predicted hold / thruplay rate as a percentage (attention proxy, Tier-A). A prediction, not a measurement.

ctr_target.description: Optional CTR target (fraction or percentage per run convention); null when no target is set.

cpa_target.description: Optional CPA target in account currency; null when the objective is not conversion-optimized (Tier-B stays unset, never invented).

muted_test_pass.description: Whether the hook reads with sound OFF (muted-first burned-in text carries the message in the first 2s). The paid-social muted-first gate.
